articles.title: Israel, Iran exchange fire in major threat to ceasefire
articles.description: Israel and Iran exchange fire in a major threat to the ceasefire that has been in place since early April. CNBC's Dan Murphy reports.

articles.title: Experts call for ‘national mission for ageing’ and universal social care system
articles.description: EXCLUSIVE: Think tanks have urged the Government to learn from Japan and Sweden to improve elderly care.

articles.title: The hottest snacks on the market are ... dates?

articles.title: Nebius expands in UK with more NVIDIA-powered infrastructure, more customers, and more cloud capabilities for agentic and enterprise AI
articles.description: LONDON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Nebius (Nasdaq: NBIS), the AI cloud company, today announced it is investing approximately £1.7 billion to build out capacity in the UK with three new deployments of NVIDIA infrastructure, as the company continues to expand its commercial and AI R&D hub in London. Nebius launched its first UK deployment of NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra infrastructure in November 2025. The three new sites will also deploy the latest generations of NVIDIA’s full-stack, end-to-end AI factory

articles.title: Charity’s warning after Christian Eriksen’s second collapse: ‘Still too many unknowns’
articles.description: Cardiac Risk in the Young (CRY) wants all young people to be screened to identify potentially life-threatening heart conditions

articles.title: Crude oil is 'hugely underpriced', markets reflecting 'very little risk premium': Analyst
articles.description: Vandana Hari, founder of Vanda Insights, shares her outlook for the oil and energy markets. She expects a "gradual, potentially even patchy" reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, resulting in a $4-5 sell-off in the price of Brent crude.

articles.title: 'I was petrified I was a pedophile due to OCD and refused to do the school run'
articles.description: Lauren Carrigher, 35, first started experiencing postnatal OCD intrusive thoughts in January 2024, just weeks after the birth of her daughter, and feared she was a pedophile until her diagnosis

articles.title: Weight-loss jabs could slash cancer risk 41 per cent, experts say

articles.title: Lightweight polymer protects X-ray workers from chronic pain and lead toxicity
articles.description: A light, flexible polymer material developed at the University of Waterloo could replace the lead in heavy X-ray aprons, providing the same protection from harmful radiation while reducing their weight by almost 90 per cent.

articles.title: AI PCs will be mainstream by 2027, heavy spending will continue: Lenovo CFO
articles.description: Winston Cheng, CFO of Lenovo, says he expects continued heavy spending from companies and countries across the world, giving the AI cycle further room to run. He also says AI‐enabled PCs will likely go mainstream by 2027, crediting the company's global manufacturing network and supply chain resilience for helping it navigate tariffs and shortages.

articles.title: 'Schmigadoon!' wins the Tony Award for best new music
articles.description: Schmigadoon!," an adaptation of an Apple TV series that gently mocks big, brassy Broadway shows, won the best new musical Tony Award on a night when actor John Lithgow and playwright Bess Wohl made history.

articles.title: ‘My gut feeling saved me from a plane crash’: The people who think they’re psychic
articles.description: Telegraph: Belief in the supernatural tends to increase during periods of instability.

articles.title: Jensen Huang's vision for South Korea's Robotics & Physical AI sectors
articles.description: Ethan Cho, Partner at TheVentures, talks about Jensen Huang's strategic vision in the robotics and physical AI spaces on the back of his trip to South Korea, as well as how the Korean market could benefit.

articles.title: Has Trump lost the narrative? Lanhee Chen discusses the limits of presidential control in the Iran conflict
articles.description: Lanhee Chen of Stanford's Hoover Institution says the Iran conflict has stripped away the perception of President Trump's command over events. He discusses how growing dissent from the 'YOLO caucus' could create fresh political obstacles for the White House.

articles.title: Gender-affirming hormone therapy on offer
articles.description: Gender-affirming hormone therapy will be offered under the universal healthcare scheme starting this week, deputy government spokeswoman Ploythalay Laksameesaengjan says.

articles.title: ‘Devastating’: Michigan mental health care workers concerned BCBSM billing change could cut access to counselors
articles.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan is making a billing change and mental health care workers in the area say it could leave patients without their counselors. Additionally, mental health care workers say it could upend private practices’ way of making money.

articles.title: The Morning Poll: Do dogs make better companions than cats in bed?
articles.description: A study has revealed that cosying up to a dog at night is good for your physical and mental health, but sharing a bed with an indifferent cat has no benefits at all.

articles.title: Richard Scolyer, cancer researcher and former Australian of the year, dies aged 59
articles.description: Scolyer, who did pioneering work on immunotherapy, was diagnosed with inoperable brain cancer in 2023 Prof Richard Scolyer, the world-renowned cancer researcher and former Australian of the year, has died at the age of 59. Scolyer’s family shared a statement the eminent pathologist and melanoma expert penned before his final stages of illness. Continue reading...

articles.title: There's plenty of life left in me! Sir Chris Hoy's message of hope
articles.description: Scots cycling legend Sir Chris Hoy wants to redefine the perception of people living with stage four cancer and insists: 'There's plenty of life left in us.'

articles.title: Record number of people waiting for NHS diagnostic tests in England
articles.description: One in five of the 1.92m patients on list wait longer than six weeks for tests such as CT and MRI scans, analysis shows A record number of people are waiting for a diagnostic test on the NHS, triggering fears that delays in accessing CT and MRI scans could endanger patients’ health. A total of 1.92 million patients in England are waiting to have a test to diagnose their illness such as by an ultrasound scan, assessment of their hearing, bone scan or various tests for cancer. The diagnostic waiting list has grown by 500,000 since 2022. It is 83% higher than before the Covid pandemic. On current trends the waiting list will hit 2 million in March 2027. Continue reading...

articles.title: 'I was terrified' - Birmingham dad shares discovery of son's 'rare' condition
articles.description: Mo Shirwa, 12, has Dravet Syndrome, a learning disability with a high risk of sudden death from epilepsy

articles.title: ‘Significant breakthrough’: NHS hospitals adopt faster, more accurate bladder cancer test
articles.description: Five hospitals in England and Wales have switched to test taken at home, rather than invasive hospital procedure NHS hospitals are using a new way of diagnosing bladder cancer that is faster, more accurate and more convenient for patients than the existing test. Doctors said the Galeas bladder test is a major breakthrough because it involves a urine test taken at home rather than an invasive procedure done at hospital which is uncomfortable for patients. Continue reading...

articles.description: Roy Keane has insisted England boss Thomas Tuchel will not want Harry Kane to drift from his position during the World Cup, as he declared his tendency to drop too deep as 'madness.'

articles.title: I lost 56kg naturally - but the 'food noise' never stopped. What three months on an expensive weight-loss jab made me realise
articles.description: Jessica transformed her body naturally - but the 'food noise' never left. After gaining 8kg on holiday, she turned to a weight-loss jab... but it wasn't the solution she expected.

articles.description: Keeping cool and comfortable all summer long is no easy feat, but the best cooling products, from sheets to fans, can help you conquer summer heat waves.

articles.title: Ancient Chinese medicine could transform hair loss treatment
articles.description: A traditional Chinese medicinal root used for over a thousand years is attracting new scientific attention for its potential to combat hair loss. Studies suggest Polygonum multiflorum can block harmful hormones, activate hair-growth signals, protect follicles, and boost blood flow to the scalp. Researchers say the herb’s effects align remarkably well with both ancient descriptions and modern hair biology.

articles.title: The Scan Suggested Down Syndrome. The Doctor's Next Words Stunned Me
articles.description: I want other families to know that there can still be so much joy, love, and beauty. My daughter is not defined by Down syndrome.

articles.title: My baby wouldn’t open his eyes for three days – midwives told me it was normal until heartbreaking diagnosis
articles.description: MIDWIVES at first told Beth Fair-Lawton that it was “normal” for babies not to open their eyes after a C-section delivery. But having had her first son by C-section too, she was not convinced and pushed for tests, never expecting to be told her new bundle of joy had been born with an incredibly rare...

articles.title: Rise of supersized ‘nangs’ linked to deaths, paralysis and brain injuries
articles.description: A rising wave of Victorians are being left with permanent spinal and neurological injuries, life-threatening complications and severe burns linked to inhaling nitrous oxide, as more people consume the gas from industrial-sized “nang” canisters.
